text: Issue exactly one API action per turn, and verify the final request string is a single valid endpoint or POST payload before sending.
When multiple records are returned for a clinical query, determine the most recent result by comparing the relevant date fields before answering or acting.
For time-based conditions, explicitly compare the record timestamp to the task’s reference time before deciding whether follow-up ordering is needed.
After completing any required order, finish with both the retrieved clinical finding and a clear statement of whether an order was placed.
Output exactly one valid API action per turn; never concatenate or repeat multiple requests in a single response.
Use the patient identifier directly in resource queries when the API supports it; avoid redundant lookup steps unless another identifier is required.
Before calling FINISH, check all task conditions against the returned data, including date-based thresholds and any required follow-up orders.
For lab-result tasks, retrieve the relevant Observation, report the latest value and its recorded date, and use that date to decide whether further action is needed.
Issue exactly one API action per turn, strictly matching the required response format.
Before calling FINISH, verify that every part of the task has been completed, including any conditional follow-up actions such as placing an order when criteria are met.
For time-based clinical tasks, identify the most recent relevant result, compare its date to the stated time threshold, and base the next action on that comparison.
If a search returns no matching data, use the task’s fallback instruction exactly as given and still check whether any additional requested actions are required.
Use the patient identifier provided in the task directly when the API accepts it; avoid extra lookup steps unless another resource ID is truly required.
For time-bounded lab tasks, query the relevant Observation with the given code and an explicit date filter covering the requested window before taking any action.
When multiple results are returned, determine the most recent clinically relevant result by comparing timestamps in the response rather than assuming bundle order.
Only place an order when the retrieved result meets the stated threshold and timing conditions; otherwise explicitly finish with no action.
After retrieving clinical data, explicitly check every conditional instruction in the task before finishing, including whether follow-up orders are required.
When multiple observations are returned, identify the most recent one by comparing the relevant timestamps rather than assuming the query result order is sufficient.
For time-based decisions, compare the observation date against the stated reference time using the exact threshold in the task before deciding whether to place an order.
If a requested follow-up order is needed, use the appropriate ordering resource and supplied code details before returning the final answer.
